run_summary: Screen 2 has one open position (MOD) whose print is 5 days away with intact thesis; hold through earnings. Four new flags arrived: two OVERDONE (MDB, ASAN) are SKIPs by structure; one OVERDONE (VSCO) is a SKIP by structure; one UNDERDONE (MOD) duplicates the open position, so SKIP on add.

entries.name: MongoDB
entries.thesis: Filings show Atlas consumption deceleration in the existing base (the engine of the model), and the company is simultaneously absorbing a CEO transition and the announced departure of the head of Field Operations — a combination that typically produces conservative first-quarter-under-new-CEO guidance and elevated execution risk on enterprise expansion. Market sentiment around MDB has historically swung on Atlas commentary; a soft or 'reset' guide is plausible and not fully priced.
entries.what_kills: Atlas re-acceleration commentary, raised FY guide, evidence Voyage AI / AI workload monetization is offsetting core deceleration, or net expansion rate stable/up versus prior quarter.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE classification — long-only book has no way to express this. Screen 2 cannot short. Filings evidence is strong (Atlas deceleration disclosed, CEO/field-ops turnover, conservative guide risk) and the 60% hit rate on OVERDONE flags is legitimate, but the structural constraint blocks entry.

entries.name: Victoria's Secret & Co.
entries.thesis: The filings explicitly tell investors that tariffs cost ~$85M in FY25 net of mitigation and that the path forward depends on factors outside management's control (Section 122 tariffs, retaliation, mitigation lead times). Mall-anchored apparel retailers with new finance leadership and an explicit tariff disclosure tend to guide cautiously on the first print of the new fiscal year. The market may be under-weighting how persistent the gross-margin pressure could be into Q1/Q2.
entries.what_kills: Tariff refunds from IEEPA reversal materially recognized, gross margin expansion, raised FY guide, strong PINK or Beauty momentum offsetting the tariff drag.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE classification — long-only book has no way to express this. Filings evidence is material (tariff headwind $85M quantified in 10-K, new CFO/CMO, margin pressure likely) but Screen 2 cannot take short positions.

entries.name: Asana
entries.thesis: Filings show retention deterioration (NRR below 100% on the highest-spend cohorts) coexisting with a heavy narrative pivot to AI/Agentic Enterprise that is not yet quantified as revenue contribution. Combined with executive departures and a disclosed security flaw in an AI-adjacent feature, the next print is more likely to surface continued NRR pressure and elevated AI investment than to deliver an upside catalyst — particularly if guidance was raised in Q3 on AI optimism.
entries.what_kills: NRR rebounds above 100%; a specific, quantified AI Studio/AI Teammates revenue contribution disclosure; clean reaffirmation of FY27 with strong large-customer growth (817 over-100K customers expanding).
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE classification — long-only book has no way to express this. Filings show NRR sub-100% on high-value cohorts, recent executive departures, and unquantified AI revenue risk, but the structure prohibits short expression.

entries.name: Modine Manufacturing
entries.thesis: Filings show a company whose mix has decisively shifted to the AI-data-center-cooling vertical (25% of sales and growing), with management announcing a transaction that will divest the weak Performance Technologies drag. The market may still be valuing MOD on the legacy mixed-industrial multiple while the filings show a clear path to a focused data-center cooling pure-play. Year-end print is the last one before the spin reframing takes hold.
entries.what_kills: Data Center Cooling growth materially decelerating, hyperscaler order pushouts, RMT transaction delay or termination, or Climate Solutions margin compression from capacity-expansion costs outpacing revenue.
entries.reasoning: Ticker already held in portfolio (5 shares, cost basis 252.282, current price 250.11, days_held 0). Screen 2 does not support ADD; averaging into a position inside the 4-day event window violates discipline. Hold existing position through T+1.
